title: Enicosanthum grandifolium
taxonomy: Enicosanthum grandifolium belongs to the family Annonaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. Within this family, Enicosanthum grandifolium is classified as a genus of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The specific classification of Enicosanthum grandifolium as a species is still a subject of ongoing research and classification updates. The genus Enicosanthum is closely related to other genera within the Annonaceae family, including Polyalthia and Pseudoxandra. Further studies are required to confirm the exact classification of Enicosanthum grandifolium within the Annonaceae family.

intro: Enicosanthum grandifolium, also known as the large-leaved Enicosanthum, is a plant species belonging to the Annonaceae family. The leaves and stems of E. grandifolium have been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, E. grandifolium is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea, as well as being made into a poultice or paste, to treat a range of health conditions.
